reason: The report explicitly identifies the burnout risk as high, noting that anesthesiologists consistently rank near the top of physician burnout surveys due to intense production pressure and high-stakes legal liability. Practitioners face extreme mental stamina demands and a moderate-to-strenuous physical toll that often results in chronic back and neck pain. Furthermore, frequent exposure to acute trauma and emergency scenarios requires significant emotional resilience, even though long-term sustainability can sometimes be achieved by shifting to outpatient settings later in the career.
